paper.title: Concrete made from ceramic industry waste: durability properties
paper.authors: RM. Senthamarai; P. Devadas Manoharan; D. Gobinath
paper.material_system: ceramic electrical insulator waste aggregate concrete
paper.notes: No direct mixture-level compressive-strength values were readable in the supplied packet; routed to review.

evidence.evidence_text: n- crete can also be called as ‘Green’ concrete. Concrete made with ceramic electrical insulator waste as coarse aggregate shows good workability, compressive, tensile and ﬂexural strengths and modulus of elasticity. Further, study of its durability will ensure greater reliability in its usage. Permeation characters are used widely to quantify durability properties of concrete.
